mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate directly reduces aggregate demand and corporate profitability, leading to lower equity prices as investors discount weaker earnings. The level of UNRATE serves as a persistent drag on forward returns independent of recent changes.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between UNRATE level and subsequent 20-day returns is non-negative over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal is not monotonic in the top vs bottom deciles of UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, the cost of capital becomes restrictive enough to dampen investment and consumption, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 5%, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapidly rising unemployment rate signals an abrupt weakening in the labor market that surprises markets and triggers downward earnings revisions, causing negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: The signal produces positive average 20-day returns in more than 40% of triggered months, or the Rank IC over the full sample is below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate a broad-based economic contraction that sharply reduces corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and RSAFS decline month-over-month (i.e., MoM change < 0)
failure_condition: When both proxies decline, the average 20-day return is not negative with statistical significance (p < 0.10), or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with rising unemployment create a stagflationary macro environment that erodes both earnings growth and valuation multiples, producing strongly negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 25bp over 3 months AND UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Under the defined trigger, the average 60-day return is not negative at the 5% significance level, or the signal does not rank in the bottom decile of all triggers in a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions (real GDP growing), rising housing starts signal strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which boosts forward equity returns. The signal flips or weakens during recessions.
failure_condition: During expansion regimes, the Spearman rank correlation between HOUST level (or change) and 20-day returns is non-positive, or the signal underperforms a simple long-only baseline in those regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index indicates expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: The weekly Rank IC of INDPRO changes on 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window, or the first-quintile minus fifth-quintile spread is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation forces the central bank to maintain a restrictive monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY > 3%, the average 20-day return is not negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate is below 50%.
